title: Senate Advances Bipartisan Infrastructure Package Amid Budget Debate
description: The Senate moved forward Thursday on a bipartisan infrastructure deal, with 68 senators voting to proceed to debate on the legislation that would invest billions in roads, bridges, and broadband.

title: White House Pushes Back on Congressional Budget Cuts to Social Programs
description: Administration officials said Sunday they would oppose House Republican efforts to cut funding for Medicare and Medicaid as part of a budget reconciliation bill.

title: House Judiciary Committee Approves Immigration Enforcement Legislation
description: The House Judiciary Committee voted along party lines to advance a sweeping immigration enforcement bill that would expand detention capacity and increase deportation funding.

title: Federal Reserve Chair Testifies Before Senate Banking Committee on Inflation
description: Federal Reserve Chair testified that inflation remains above the 2% target and signaled that interest rate cuts may be delayed until economic conditions stabilize further.

title: Democrats Introduce Comprehensive Climate Bill With Carbon Pricing Mechanism
description: A coalition of Senate and House Democrats unveiled sweeping climate legislation Friday that would establish a national carbon price and invest in clean energy manufacturing.

title: Supreme Court Takes Up Major Social Media and First Amendment Case
description: The Supreme Court agreed to hear a challenge to state laws limiting how social media platforms can moderate content, a case with major implications for free speech online.

title: Pentagon Budget Request Includes Major AI and Drone Investment
description: The Department of Defense submitted its fiscal year 2027 budget request to Congress, seeking significant increases in artificial intelligence development and unmanned systems.

title: Senate Republicans Block Voting Rights Legislation for Third Time
description: Senate Republicans used a filibuster to block the Freedom to Vote Act, a Democratic-backed bill that would set national standards for voter registration and early voting.

title: Biden Signs Executive Order on AI Regulation and Safety Standards
description: The administration issued a sweeping executive order directing federal agencies to develop standards for AI safety, security, and transparency across government operations.

title: House Passes Farm Bill With Expanded SNAP Benefits After Months of Negotiations
description: The House passed a five-year farm bill that includes modest expansions to food assistance programs, clearing a major legislative hurdle after two years of stalled negotiations.

title: Senate Confirms New Attorney General in Narrow Party-Line Vote
description: The Senate confirmed the president's pick for Attorney General by a 51-49 margin, with all Republicans voting against in one of the most contentious confirmation battles in recent memory.

title: GOP Leadership Unveils Tax Reform Framework Ahead of Budget Deadline
description: House Republican leaders released a tax reform outline that would extend the 2017 tax cuts and add new deductions for small businesses, estimating a $4 trillion impact over 10 years.

title: Bipartisan Group Reaches Deal on Health Care Cost Transparency Rules
description: A bipartisan group of senators announced an agreement on legislation that would require hospitals and insurers to publicly disclose negotiated prices, aiming to reduce surprise medical bills.

title: Congress Debates Reauthorization of Section 702 Surveillance Powers
description: Lawmakers are at odds over whether to reauthorize a controversial surveillance program that allows the government to collect foreign intelligence communications without a warrant.

title: Treasury Department Proposes New Rules on Corporate Tax Inversions
description: The Treasury Department released proposed rules aimed at curtailing corporate tax inversions, transactions in which U.S. companies reincorporate abroad to lower their tax bills.

title: House Armed Services Committee Approves Troop Pay Increase in NDAA Markup
description: The House Armed Services Committee approved a 4.5% pay raise for military personnel as part of its markup of the National Defense Authorization Act.

title: Medicaid Work Requirements Return in House Republican Budget Plan
description: House Republicans are advancing a budget proposal that would require able-bodied Medicaid recipients to work or participate in job training as a condition of receiving benefits.

title: Senate Foreign Relations Committee Approves New Ukraine Aid Package
description: The Senate Foreign Relations Committee advanced a $12 billion supplemental aid package for Ukraine, including military assistance and economic support.

title: FTC Challenges Major Grocery Chain Merger Over Antitrust Concerns
description: The Federal Trade Commission filed a lawsuit to block a proposed $25 billion merger between two of the nation's largest supermarket chains, citing concerns about higher food prices.

title: Lawmakers Introduce Legislation to Ban Congressional Stock Trading
description: A bipartisan group of House and Senate members introduced a bill that would ban members of Congress and their spouses from trading individual stocks while in office.
